# Generated Files

This document lists all auto-generated files in mara_host.
**DO NOT EDIT THESE FILES DIRECTLY** - they will be overwritten by generators.

## Regenerate All

```bash
mara generate all
# or
python -m mara_host.tools.generate_all
```

## Generated Files by Generator

### gen_commands.py ‚Üí Command Definitions

| File | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `config/client_commands.py` | `MaraCommandsMixin` with `cmd_*()` methods |
| `config/command_defs.py` | `CommandDef` dataclasses |
| `config/commands.json` | Command metadata JSON |

**Source of truth:** `tools/schema/commands/` (12 domain files, 79 commands)

### gen_binary_commands.py ‚Üí Binary Protocol

| File | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `command/binary_commands.py` | `Opcode` enum class |
| `command/json_to_binary.py` | `JsonToBinaryEncoder` for high-rate commands |

**Source of truth:** `tools/schema/binary.py`

### gen_telemetry.py ‚Üí Telemetry Sections

| File | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `telemetry/telemetry_sections.py` | `TELEM_*` section ID constants |

**Source of truth:** `tools/schema/telemetry.py`

### gen_version.py ‚Üí Version Constants

| File | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `config/version.py` | `PROTOCOL_VERSION`, `SCHEMA_VERSION`, etc. |

**Source of truth:** `tools/schema/version.py`

### gen_can.py ‚Üí CAN Bus Definitions

| File | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `transport/can_defs_generated.py` | CAN message IDs and structures |

**Source of truth:** `tools/schema/can.py`

### generate_pins.py ‚Üí Pin Configuration

| File | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `config/pin_config.py` | Pin definitions for firmware |
| `config/pins.json` | Pin assignment metadata |

**Source of truth:** User assignments via `mara pins assign`

### gpio_mapping_gen.py ‚Üí GPIO Channels

| File | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `config/gpio_channels.py` | GPIO channel mappings |
| `config/gpio_channels.json` | Channel metadata JSON |

**Source of truth:** `tools/schema/gpio_channels.py`

## Schema Package Structure

The schema package (`tools/schema/`) is the single source of truth:

```
tools/schema/
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ __init__.py         # Public exports
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ commands/           # Command definitions by domain
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ _safety.py      # Safety/state machine (9 commands)
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ _rates.py       # Loop rates (4 commands)
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ _control.py     # Signal bus, slots (13 commands)
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ _motion.py      # Velocity commands (2 commands)
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ _gpio.py        # LED, GPIO, PWM (7 commands)
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ _servo.py       # Servo (3 commands)
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ _stepper.py     # Stepper (3 commands)
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ _sensors.py     # Encoder, ultrasonic (5 commands)
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ _dc_motor.py    # DC motor + PID (5 commands)
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ _observer.py    # Luenberger observer (6 commands)
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ _telemetry.py   # Telemetry config (2 commands)
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ _camera.py      # ESP32-CAM (20 commands)
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ binary.py           # BINARY_COMMANDS
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ telemetry.py        # TELEMETRY_SECTIONS
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ version.py          # VERSION, CAPABILITIES
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ can.py              # CAN_* definitions
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ gpio_channels.py    # GPIO_CHANNELS
‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ pins.py             # PINS (loads pins.json)
```

## How to Identify Generated Files

Generated files contain one of these headers:

```python
# AUTO-GENERATED - DO NOT EDIT
```

```python
# Generated by gen_commands.py - DO NOT EDIT
```

## When to Regenerate

Regenerate after:
- Editing any file in `tools/schema/commands/`
- Editing `tools/schema/binary.py`
- Editing `tools/schema/telemetry.py`
- Editing `tools/schema/version.py`
- Editing `tools/schema/can.py`
- Editing `tools/schema/gpio_channels.py`
- Adding/removing commands
- Changing protocol version
- Modifying pin assignments

## Adding New Generated Files

1. Create generator in `tools/gen_*.py`
2. Add source data to appropriate file in `tools/schema/`
3. Add to `tools/generate_all.py`
4. Document in this file
5. Add header comment to generated output
